title: Pleurothallis correllii
taxonomy: Pleurothallis correllii is a species of orchid, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Epidendroideae, to which Pleurothallis correllii is assigned. Within the Epidendroideae subfamily, Pleurothallis correllii is classified in the tribe Maxillarieae. The genus Pleurothallis itself is a part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. The exact classification of Pleurothallis correllii may vary depending on the source.

intro: Pleurothallis correllii, also known as the Coralroot Pleurothallis, is a species of orchid native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis correllii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pleurothallis correllii is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions.
